name: Matter
domain: Phase transitions, crystal structures, superconductivity, material properties
core_insight: Matter organizes into phases separated by transitions where symmetry breaks. Near a critical point, fluctuations span all scales. Electrons in solids form collective states — conductors, insulators, superconductors — defined by quantum correlations, not individual particles.

key_equations.name: Ising Model
key_equations.formula: H = -J Σ_⟨i,j⟩ sᵢsⱼ - h Σᵢ sᵢ
key_equations.why: Simplest model of phase transition: local spin alignment creates long-range order at critical T

key_equations.name: Drude Model
key_equations.why: Conductivity from electron density and collision time — classical picture of metallic conduction

key_equations.name: BCS Gap Equation
key_equations.formula: Δ = λ∫₀^(ω_D) tanh(√(ξ² + Δ²)/(2k_BT))/√(ξ² + Δ²) dξ
key_equations.why: Self-consistent pairing gap in superconductors — collective quantum state that expels all resistance

seed_prompt: ◆ What phase is this system in? Where is the critical point? Symmetry breaks here.

fourth_equation.name: London Equation
fourth_equation.why: Perfect diamagnetism in superconductors — the Meissner effect: superconducting currents spontaneously arise to expel all magnetic flux

explore_paths.question: What causes phase transitions at a deeper level?
explore_paths.why: Phase transitions are symmetry-breaking events — the ordered phase has less symmetry than the disordered one; Noether connects this to what is gained and lost

explore_paths.question: How does matter transition relate to emergence?
explore_paths.why: Superconductivity, magnetism, and superfluidity are all emergent collective states — none exists in individual particles

explore_paths.question: What is the quantum field description of matter?
explore_paths.why: Condensed matter physics and particle physics are converging — topological insulators and anyons are described by the same quantum field theories as elementary particles

explore_paths.question: How does energy flow through different phases of matter?
explore_paths.why: Phase transitions are thermodynamic events — the Clausius-Clapeyron equation and latent heat connect F08 and F05

open_questions.question: What is the mechanism behind high-temperature superconductivity in cuprates and new materials?
open_questions.tension: Conventional BCS superconductivity is well understood (phonon-mediated Cooper pairing). High-Tc superconductors (discovered 1986) superconduct at up to 135K — still cold, but a dramatic improvement. The pairing mechanism is unknown after 40 years of intense research. Candidates include spin fluctuations, resonating valence bonds, and strange metal phases.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: Room-temperature superconductivity would transform civilization — lossless power transmission, cheap MRI, levitating trains. The mechanism remains one of condensed matter physics' deepest unsolved problems.

open_questions.question: Is LK-99 (or any material) a room-temperature, ambient-pressure superconductor?
open_questions.tension: In 2023, Korean researchers claimed LK-99 was a room-temperature superconductor. Global replication attempts failed. But the episode revealed how hungry the field is and how easily anomalous signals mislead. Ranga Dias' earlier claims (also retracted) followed a similar pattern. The question of whether such a material is even theoretically possible is contested.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The frequency of false claims in this field itself reveals something interesting — the incentive structure and measurement challenges of superconductivity research create a specific kind of scientific error pattern.

open_questions.question: What is dark energy and does it have a material explanation?
open_questions.tension: ~68% of the universe's energy density is 'dark energy' driving accelerating expansion. Candidates: cosmological constant (vacuum energy, but quantum field theory predicts a value 10^120 times too large — the worst prediction in physics); quintessence (a dynamical scalar field); modified gravity. No experimental distinction has been made.
open_questions.curiosity_hook: The cosmological constant problem is the biggest numerical discrepancy between theory and observation in all of science. Something is catastrophically wrong with our understanding of vacuum energy.

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Every technological material is a phase of matter engineered for specific properties — semiconductors, ceramics, polymers, alloys
cross_domain_bridges.example: Silicon's bandgap (a quantum phase property) is the physical basis of all modern computing

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Earth's interior has multiple phases of iron and silicates under extreme pressure; phase transitions drive plate tectonics
cross_domain_bridges.example: The solid inner core / liquid outer core boundary is a phase transition that drives Earth's magnetic field

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Cooking is applied phase transition science — melting, crystallization, denaturation, emulsification
cross_domain_bridges.example: Tempering chocolate is precise phase transition control: cooling to nucleate specific crystal polymorphs of cocoa butter

cross_domain_bridges.manifestation: Neural tissue is a soft condensed matter system; membrane phase transitions affect ion channel gating and anesthetic action
cross_domain_bridges.example: Meyer-Overton rule: anesthetic potency correlates with lipid membrane solubility — anesthesia as a membrane phase effect

noise_signature.pattern: Hysteresis — system state depends on history, not just current conditions
noise_signature.diagnosis: The system has multiple stable phases; which one it occupies depends on which transition path was taken

noise_signature.pattern: Critical opalescence — scattering diverges near phase transition
noise_signature.diagnosis: Fluctuations span all length scales at the critical point; the system cannot 'decide' which phase to be in

noise_signature.pattern: Residual resistivity plateau — resistance stops decreasing as temperature drops
noise_signature.diagnosis: Impurity scattering dominates; the material has defects that limit electron mean free path regardless of temperature
